IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Thursday, July 16, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is running hot with **$54.75 top scale** and serious overscale work. Ermco's Google Data Center gig in Mooresville is OSHA 10 required—solid long-term call. Meade and Fluor are both paying **10% above scale** on Meta and Eli Lilly work in Lebanon, running 6-10s Mon-Sat. Book depth sits around 195 hands for 46 calls; if you're OSHA-certified, you slot in quick.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** has four contractors stacked on the Port Washington Data Center project, all offering **JW wage plus $10/hr**—that's real money. Gurtz, MJ Electric, and Cupertino are all Wisconsin-license-gated, 50–60 hr/wk commitments. This is where the mega-infrastructure work landed this quarter.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** is the anomaly: **$65.33 scale** but Micron jobs paying only **$45.33/hr** under PLA agreement with **$170/day per diem** if eligible. VECA, Quality, and EC Electric are all hiring; the per diem sweetens the deal for out-of-state travelers. 4-10s, day shift starts at 6am.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** keeps steady at **$48.00 top scale** with Inglett & Stubbs running the volume—40 calls total, ~225 hands needed. Cable splicer work at QTS Fayette, hospital rough-in at Wellstar Paulding. Book depth is manageable; 4-10s and 2-8s split schedules are standard.

**LU-237 Niagara Falls, NY** has thin calls (8 total) but **per diem available** on Ferguson and O'Connell's Lake Mariner Data Center work. First shift, multiple orientation days. Low board-to-hand ratio makes entry faster here.

**LU-26 Washington, DC** shows 48 calls but only 151 hands—**the tightest ratio on the board**—with data center testing work paying NFPA 70E and MOP certifications into the rate.

Data center buildout is pulling hands north and west; Midwest (Milwaukee, Indianapolis) and East Coast (DC, Niagara) are the current magnets.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Thursday, July 16, 2026**

**LU-71 Columbus, OH** is running hot with 40 calls and only ~124 hands on the bench. NEW RIVER ELECT. CORP. has 36 JW spots on Columbus distribution work at **$61.44/hr** with **$125 night per diem**, a **$100 daily bonus for full attendance**, and 6-10s. Same contractor's pushing 9 more JWs into Southern Ohio on 4-10s optional Friday/Sat. Grab it—book depth is tight and the incentive structure rewards steady hands.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** is printing work. Four separate calls from VECA Electric, Quality Electric, and EC Electric—55 inside JW positions at **$45.33/hr** on the Micron Intel PLA, with **$170/day per diem for seven days** if eligible and a **$20/hr incentive** on certain scopes. Quality and EC are running 5-8s; VECA mixed 4-10s and day shift. Toxic gas monitoring and assembly work. Book sits at ~119 hands.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** has 25 calls chasing only ~90 hands. Parsons Electric owns the board with dual 22-call and 21-call lines at the Polaris Forge Data Center in Harwood. **$44.80/hr** base plus **$100/day incentive Monday–Friday, $150 Saturday/Sunday**, and **$100/day non-taxed per diem**. 100+ mile GPS radius. Hunt Electric's offering 10 more JW spots at Grand Forks Air Base. This is premium overtime country—data center construction always eats hours.

**LU-1547 Anchorage, AK** sits at 47 calls against 60 hands—the loosest book here—but Golden Valley, Carlos Tree, and Lineworks are hiring. Outside telecom and tree trim work **$43.55–$54.53/hr** with Lineworks running 6-10s. Alaska pay doesn't compete with the Lower 48, but the work's steady.

**LU-474 Memphis, TN** and **LU-175 Chattanooga, TN** are data-center magnets: ArchKey Sachs and Lawson Electric pulling Inside JWs at **$36.65–$31.40/hr** (with 31.4% night shift differential in Chattanooga) on 4-12s and 6-10s. Thin books but consistent calls.

**The intel:** Data-center work is absorbing half the board—Boise, Memphis, Chattanooga, Grand Forks—while traditional distro and transmission remains steady in Columbus and the mountain west. Hands chasing semis and incentives, not base scale.

UA.text:
**Trampin the UA Locals — Thursday, July 16, 2026**

**LU-140 Salt Lake City, UT** is running hot with 28 calls and 105 hands needed — the widest board today. BCS Mechanical alone has 34 Journeyman Plumber slots on West Valley work, plus 10 apprentice spots if you're entry-level. P.C. has 7 more on the Delta Center Salt Lake City project. DSI's looking for Journeyman Pipefitters who can tig tack at their Draper shop. Book depth is real here; steady work, no per diem crunch.

**LU-208 Denver, CO** is tighter — 21 calls, only 34 hands in the system — which means you clear faster. Kiewit's Fort St. Vrain Powerhouse is the anchor: **$65/day per diem**, nights 5/9's (9.5 hours), Welder slot. US Eng. Metalworks has 4 CS MIG welder spots on nights (5/10's, 4pm–2:15am). Murphy's fab shop is running 6/10 nights with shift differential. Real money if you're a graveyard guy.

**LU-213 St. John, NB** has 9 calls chasing 34 hands on Irving Oil Refinery work — day-shift 4/10's across multiple contractors. Stuart Olson, the main outfit, needs 2 Journeyman Pipefitters under GPMA A. Lorneville runs refinery *and* pulp mill spreads. Cam's pulling both fitters and F3/F4 test welders. Maritime work, stable contractual agreements, no travel surprises.

**LU-290 Portland, OR** (22 calls, 24 hands) is Intel-heavy: Apollo's got 2 BIM Detailer slots at Ronler Acres Tigard on Foreman scale. Charter and Harder are pulling detailers and specialty welders (PVDF PP IR). PermaCold's commercial refrigeration service calls are the volume play. Tech skills premium here.

**LU-192 Cheyenne, WY** punches above weight: only 3 calls but MAP's Holly Frontier Sinclair Refinery shutdown is pulling **12 welders** (night shift differential 10%), and PMI's got **$100/day per diem** on Ellsworth AFB (Box Elder, SD) — 1 combo welder, 1 fitter, 3 plumbers. South Dakota work, federal sites, clean backgrounders only.

**LU-776 Lima, OH** (8 calls, 18 hands) is fab-shop heavy with 5 combo welder test slots across Gem, BMWC, and Nooter — all 6–10 hour nights, **5% night shift premium** minimum, some pulling **$11/hr straight time and $16.50/hr OT** within 50 miles or **$150/day per diem** beyond. Cenovus Maintenance is the maintenance anchor, 5–8s with spotty OT.

Irving Oil refinery spreads and federal fab work are swallowing bodies; East Coast and Midwest industrial shutdowns are the play right now.
